risk_if_not_implemented: Without Third-Party Criticality Assessments, third-party risks may go unmanaged, creating supply-chain exposure that compromises the organization.

compensating_control_1.name: Risk Assessment
compensating_control_1.description: Mechanisms exist to conduct recurring assessments of risk that includes the likelihood and magnitude of harm, from unauthorized access, use, disclosure, disruption, modification or destruction of the organization's Technology Assets, Applications, Services and/or Data (TAASD).
compensating_control_1.justification: Risk Assessment (RSK-04) provides periodic assessment and assurance that compensates for the absence of Third-Party Criticality Assessments (TPM-02) by providing structured evaluation of the environment to surface gaps and verify residual control effectiveness. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.

compensating_control_2.name: Third-Party Contract Requirements
compensating_control_2.description: Mechanisms exist to require contractual requirements for applicable security, compliance and resilience requirements with third-parties, reflecting the organization's needs to protect its Technology Assets, Applications, Services and/or Data (TAASD).
compensating_control_2.justification: Third-Party Contract Requirements (TPM-05) provides third-party oversight that compensates for the absence of Third-Party Criticality Assessments (TPM-02) by extending security obligations contractually and monitoring third-party risk in lieu of direct control. Given the process-oriented nature of this control, this compensating control targets the same underlying risk objective through an alternative approach, helping to maintain an acceptable level of residual risk until the primary control can be implemented.
